label: Aerial Survey Count
definition: Overflights (helicopter, fixed wing, or drone) with documented flight lines, coverage, visibility, and sensor settings. Imagery may be reviewed post-flight.

label: Age 1 year class
definition: Age class value 1 year.

label: Age 2 year class
definition: Age class value 2 years.

label: Age 3 year class
definition: Age class value 3 years.

label: Age 4 year class
definition: Age class value 4 years.

label: Age 5 year class
definition: Age class value 5 years.

label: Age 6 year class
definition: Age class value 6 years.

label: Age 7 year class
definition: Age class value 7 years.

label: Age at maturity basis
definition: Age measured at maturity.

label: Age at return basis
definition: Age measured at return migration (adult return timing).

label: Age at sampling basis
definition: Age measured at the time of sampling or observation.

label: Age basis
definition: The reference event or life-history point that an age value is measured against.

label: Age basis scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for the reference event used by an age value (for example, age at return).

label: Age class value
definition: An integer-like age class value used as a compositional constraint in salmon measurements.

label: Age class value scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for integer-like age class values used as constraints on salmon measurements.

label: Age dimension
definition: The age component represented by a value (total age, freshwater years, or ocean years).

label: Age dimension scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for which age component is represented (total age, freshwater years, or ocean years).

label: Age notation
definition: A notation system used to encode salmon age values in stock assessment data.

label: Age notation scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for salmon age notation systems (for example, Gilbert-Rich and European notation).

label: Amber zone
definition: Amber status implies caution in the management of the Conservation Unit. While a CU in the Amber zone should be at a low risk of loss, there will be a degree of lost production. Decisions about the conservation of CUs in the Amber zone will involve broader consideration of biological, social, and economic issues.

label: Area Under the Curve
definition: Integrates serial counts over time using a survey-life parameter; may apply observer efficiency.

label: Benchmark Level Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for generic benchmark-level qualifiers usable as I-ADOPT constraint values attached to benchmark variables or values.

label: Biological Benchmark and Indicator Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for key biological benchmarks, performance indicators, and reference points used in salmon stock assessment and management (e.g., SMSY, Sgen, spawner abundance).

label: Brood year
definition: TThe parental year for a group of returning salmon, i.e. the calendar year when the majority of parents of these fish spawned.

label: Bypass/Breach Risk
definition: Bypass or breach risks not monitored and verified negligible

label: Calibrated Time Series
definition: Regression/state-space model calibrated to a Type-1/2 series converts a lower-precision index to abundance.

label: Catch context
definition: Measurement context indicating values refer to fishery catch.

label: Catch year
definition: The calendar year in which returning salmon are caught in fisheries (as opposed to the brood year in which the cohort was spawned).

label: Classification
definition: Classification/attribution not documented

label: Confidence category
definition: Categorical confidence rating (High, Medium, Low) for stock assessment metrics.

label: Conservation Unit
definition: A group of fish sufficiently isolated from other groups that, if extirpated, is very unlikely to recolonize naturally within an acceptable timeframe.

label: Conservation Unit of
definition: Relates a Conservation Unit to the Stock Management Unit it belongs to.

label: COSEWIC Status Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for COSEWIC statuses

label: Cross-section Coverage
definition: Incomplete cross-section coverage

label: Data quality rating context
definition: Measurement context indicating categorical or ordinal quality ratings.

label: Data, Models, and Provenance
definition: Data models, analytical workflows, provenance, quality dimensions, and metadata supporting salmon science.

label: Deme
definition: A local interbreeding group of organisms within a species. In salmon, a deme typically corresponds to a spawning population at a specific site. Demes are the finest biological unit and make up populations.

label: deme of
definition: Relates a deme to the population it belongs to.

label: Detectability
definition: Detectability factors not validated

label: Device Configuration
definition: Device not within operational specifications

label: Documentation
definition: Missing SIL/SEN logs or QA/methods report

label: Downgrade Criteria
definition: Criteria that may result in downgrading an escapement estimate type classification

label: Downgrade Criteria Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for estimate type downgrade criteria

label: Effort Standardization
definition: Effort not standardized

label: Electrofishing Count
definition: Effort-standardized electrofishing passes used to generate catch-per-unit-effort (CPUE) indices.

label: Enumeration Method
definition: A method used to enumerate or count salmon in the field

label: Environmental Conditions
definition: Environmental conditions outside specifications

label: Escapement
definition: The number of mature salmon that pass through (or escape) fisheries and return to fresh water to spawn.

label: Escapement Enumeration Method Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for salmon enumeration methods

label: Escapement Estimate Method Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for salmon estimate methods

label: Escapement Estimate Type Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for Hyatt 1997 estimate types

label: Escapement measurement
definition: A measurement or estimate of escapement tied to a stock and a survey event.

label: Escapement Survey Event
definition: A survey event specifically for estimating escapement.

label: Estimate Method
definition: A method used to analyze and estimate salmon abundance from enumeration data

label: Estimate Type
definition: Classification of escapement estimate quality based on Hyatt 1997 framework

label: European age notation
definition: Salmon age notation that records freshwater years followed by ocean years (for example, 1.2).

label: Expansion/Mathematical Operations
definition: Applies expansion factors or arithmetic transforms to a base count/index.

label: Exploitation rate
definition: Expressed as a percentage, the proportion of the total return of adult salmon in a given year that die as a result of fishing activity.

label: Fish Stocks Provisions
definition: Legal provisions in Canada's Fisheries Act and regulations related to maintaining and rebuilding fish stocks, including obligations triggered when reference points are breached.

label: Fisheries Management
definition: Fishery openings, harvest control rules, catch/effort measures, and exploitation monitoring for salmon fisheries.

label: Fixed Site Census (Electronic)
definition: Automated counting at a constrained opening using electronic/optical counters, resistivity counters, or video/camera systems. QA review of automated events, cross-section coverage, and uptime/coverage are documented.

label: Fixed Site Census (Manual)
definition: Salmon are guided through a controlled opening in a fence/weir where trained observers visually tally each passage event on a continuous or daily schedule. Counting chambers/windows and SIL/SEN daily logs document coverage; bypass routes are monitored and closed where possible.

label: Fixed‑Station Tally
definition: Seasonal total = roll‑up of daily/continuous counts from a fixed (non‑sonar) station; simple arithmetic with documented coverage.

label: Freshwater age dimension
definition: Age value represents years spent in freshwater.

label: Genetics and Stock Composition
definition: Genetic markers, assays, and analyses used to infer stock composition, parentage, and population structure.

label: Gilbert-Rich age notation
definition: Salmon age notation where total age is annotated with freshwater years as a subscript-like suffix (for example, 4_2 for total age 4 with 2 freshwater years).

label: Green zone
definition: Social and economic considerations will tend to be the primary drivers for the management of Conservation Units in the Green zone, though ecosystem or other non-consumptive use values could also be considered.

label: Habitat, Ecosystem, and Climate Pressures
definition: Habitat attributes, ecosystem conditions, and climate or environmental stressors affecting salmon.

label: has Conservation Unit
definition: Relates a Stock Management Unit to one of its constituent Conservation Units.

label: has deme
definition: Relates a population to one of its constituent demes.

label: has population
definition: Relates a Conservation Unit to one of its constituent populations.

label: Hatchery-origin
definition: Individuals that were born or reared in a hatchery facility.

label: High confidence
definition: Rapid status confidence category indicating that high-quality abundance metrics support the result.

label: Hydroacoustic Modelling
definition: Model pipeline converts sonar detections to passage totals; sets range/angle/blind zones; species/size attribution rules documented.

label: Hydroacoustic Sonar Count
definition: Sonar detections (ARIS, DIDSON, or other hydroacoustic systems) with documented coverage, visibility/noise conditions, and uptime. Raw detections require processing through a modelling/classification pipeline.

label: In-river phase
definition: Freshwater in-river phase (migration and/or residence) outside the marine environment.

label: Indicator river
definition: Indicator streams are those that have been identified in some Regions as providing more reliable indices of spawner abundance. Indicator streams tend to give relatively accurate annual spawner abundance estimates, given that they use higher quality methodologies and are more intensively/regularly surveyed. Indicator streams are also assumed to be representative of spawner returns to other streams in close geographic proximity.

label: Infill Method
definition: Interpolation used for missing data; defensibility must be documented

label: Life phase
definition: A phase or segment used to stratify salmon measurements by where or when they apply (e.g., ocean phase, terminal phase).

label: Life phase scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for life-phase or fishery/migration segment qualifiers used to stratify measurements (e.g., ocean, terminal, mainstem).

label: Limit reference point
definition: A fish stock experiencing serious harm may be depleted to the point where it has impaired productivity or reproductive capacity. As a result, the stock would be less resilient to fishing and could not easily rebuild. Serious harm may be irreversible, or only slowly reversible over the long-term, and can be due to fishing, other human-induced impacts, or natural causes. Under the Precautionary Approach policy and the Fish Stocks Provisions (FSPs), breaching the LRP is a trigger for a rebuilding plan.

label: Low confidence
definition: Rapid status confidence category indicating limited supporting data (for example, derived from trend metrics alone).

label: Lower benchmark
definition: A lower-threshold benchmark value, typically delineating the boundary between Red and Amber status zones.

label: Lower biological benchmark
definition: Lower benchmark value for a metric used to delineate Wild Salmon Policy status zones (typically the boundary between Red and Amber zones).

label: Mainstem phase
definition: Freshwater mainstem migration phase in a river system (often corresponding to mainstem in-river fisheries or monitoring segments).

label: Management reference context
definition: Measurement context indicating values are management benchmarks or reference-oriented metrics.

label: Mark-Recapture Analysis
definition: Capture-recapture estimators (Petersen, Jolly-Seber, open/Bayesian models) applied to field marks/recoveries.

label: Mark-Recapture Assumptions
definition: MR assumptions not evaluated

label: Mark-Recapture Field Program
definition: Field program involving marking and subsequent recapture/recovery of tagged fish with documented assumptions and recovery coverage.

label: Measurement context
definition: A context qualifier used to disambiguate the interpretation of a measurement column.

label: Measurement context scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for compositional measurement context qualifiers (for example, catch context, run context).

label: Medium confidence
definition: Rapid status confidence category indicating mixed evidence or reliance on trend metrics supplemented by some abundance information.

label: Method Unknown/Inconsistent
definition: Survey/analytical methods not adequately identified/consistent

label: Metric benchmark
definition: Benchmark value for a metric that helps delineate status zones (for example, the limit reference point for absolute abundance).

label: Monitoring and Field Work
definition: Field observations, sampling protocols, survey events, gear, and measurement methods used to collect salmon data.

label: Natural-origin
definition: Individuals that are born and reared in the wild (natural environment).

label: Number of Visits
definition: Insufficient number of survey visits

label: Observed rate or abundance
definition: A measurement datum representing an empirically observed rate, count, or abundance derived from monitoring or survey data.

label: Ocean age dimension
definition: Age value represents years spent in the marine environment.

label: Ocean phase
definition: Phase of the salmon life cycle or assessment period occurring in the marine environment.

label: Peak Count Analysis
definition: Uses the peak survey (live ± dead variants) as the season estimate/index.

label: Policy framework
definition: A policy, legal, or governance framework that defines how salmon status metrics, benchmarks, or reference points are set and interpreted.

label: Policy framework scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for policy and governance frameworks referenced by salmon assessment metrics and benchmarks (e.g., WSP, PA).

label: Policy, Governance, and Organizational Structure
definition: DFO policy frameworks, governance, regulations, and organizational structure relevant to salmon management.

label: Population
definition: A group of organisms of the same species occupying a defined area that interbreed and share a gene pool. In salmon, populations are composed of one or more demes and form the biological basis for Conservation Units.

label: population of
definition: Relates a population to the Conservation Unit it belongs to.

label: Precautionary Approach
definition: DFO policy framework for applying precaution in fisheries decision-making, including use of reference points and stock status zones.

label: Precision/Accuracy
definition: Precision/accuracy weaker than expected for type

label: Rapid status confidence scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for categorical confidence ratings attached to rapid status assessments.

label: Reach Coverage
definition: Insufficient reach coverage

label: Recruit stage context
definition: Measurement context indicating values refer to recruits.

label: Red zone
definition: A Conservation Unit in the Red zone is undesirable because of the risk of extirpation, and the loss of ecological benefits and salmon production. Changes in status will trigger management actions that will vary depending on species, geographic regions, and cause of the decline.

label: Redd Count
definition: Redd counts with documentation of detectability, timing, and reach coverage. Conversion to spawner estimates requires spawners-per-redd factors handled in analysis.

label: Redd Expansion Analysis
definition: Converts redd counts to spawner estimates using spawners-per-redd expansion factors.

label: Reference point
definition: Reference points are used as targets to achieve or as limits to avoid.

label: Reporting or management stratum
definition: An information-defined grouping of fish populations used for reporting, conservation assessment, or management purposes. Examples include Conservation Units (CUs), Stock Management Units (MUs), and Designatable Units (DUs).

label: Review/QA
definition: Insufficient QA review of automated events

label: Run context
definition: Measurement context indicating values refer to run size or returning run composition.

label: Run Coverage
definition: Run-window coverage < 95% of season days

label: Salmon Enhancement and Hatcheries
definition: Hatchery production, broodstock management, releases, and enhancement program practices for salmon.

label: Salmon origin
definition: Origin category describing whether salmon are naturally produced, hatchery produced, or mixed-origin.

label: Salmon Origin Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for describing the origin of individual salmon (e.g., natural-origin, hatchery-origin, mixed-origin).

label: Spawner stage context
definition: Measurement context indicating values refer to spawners or spawning-stage fish.

label: Species at Risk and Recovery
definition: COSEWIC/SARA recovery planning, critical habitat, threats, and status assessments for species of conservation concern.

label: Stock
definition: An operationally-defined grouping of salmon used for assessment and management purposes. A stock is a management unit or reporting stratum, not a biological organism.

label: Stock Assessment
definition: Stock assessment models, benchmarks, status determinations, and performance metrics for salmon units.

label: Stock assessment
definition: The scientific process of analyzing data to estimate the abundance and productivity of fish stocks in the past, present, and future. It is the backbone of successful science-based fisheries management

label: Stock Management Unit
definition: A collection of stocks that are managed together with a joint stock status.

label: Stock Status Zone Scheme
definition: Controlled vocabulary for stock status zones under the Precautionary Approach.

label: Survey event
definition: A field event that collects information about key fish stocks to track their status, and track progress to implement fishery policies and measures for these stocks.

label: Survival or mortality context
definition: Measurement context indicating values represent survival, mortality, or related rates.

label: Target or limit rate or abundance
definition: A value specification representing a policy- or model-defined target or limit, such as TAC or UMSY.

label: Terminal phase
definition: Phase occurring in terminal areas near river mouths or spawning areas (often including terminal fisheries and local migration to spawning sites).

label: theme
definition: Associates OWL classes and SKOS concepts with high-level thematic concept schemes (e.g., Stock Assessment, GSI, Policy).

label: Theme Scheme
definition: High-level thematic concept scheme for organizing DFO Salmon Ontology classes and concepts.

label: Timing
definition: Poor timing of surveys

label: Total age dimension
definition: Age value represents total age in years.

label: Total exploitation rate
definition: The overall proportion of the total return of adult salmon removed by all fisheries over a defined period, aggregating across gear or fishery components.

label: Trap Count
definition: Trap-based counts from non-spanning traps with documented emptying schedule, efficiency measurements, and coverage information.

label: Trap Efficiency
definition: Trap efficiency not measured/validated

label: Trap Model Analysis
definition: Trap-based counts adjusted with efficiency/coverage models to estimate total passage.

label: Type-1, True Abundance, high resolution
definition: Total, seasonal counts through fence or fishway; virtually no bypass. Simple, often single step analysis. Reliable resolution of between year differences >10% (in absolute units).

label: Type-2, True Abundance, medium resolution
definition: Indirect estimate of total spawner abundance with qualified accuracy and known precision (i.e., a variance estimate—an explicit quantitative measure of uncertainty around the estimate). Derived from high-effort, standardized methods (e.g., mark-recapture, serial counts for area under the curve) with documented analytical conversions (including interpolation/calibration as needed). Reported in absolute units (estimate ± variance). Reliable resolution of between-year differences >25% (in absolute units).

label: Type-3, Relative Abundance, high resolution
definition: Relative abundance index (i.e., not an absolute count) derived from moderate to high effort surveys (typically 3 or more site visits) using standardized methods applied consistently between and within years (e.g., equal-effort surveys executed by walk, swim, overflight). Accuracy and precision are not fully quantified (i.e., no explicit variance estimate), but are assumed stable enough for within-series comparisons. Reliable resolution of between-year differences >25% (in relative units).

label: Type-4, Relative Abundance, medium resolution
definition: Low to moderate effort (1-4 trips), known survey method. Simple analysis by known methods. Reliable resolution of between year differences >200% (in relative units).

label: Type-5, Relative Abundance, low resolution
definition: Low effort (e.g. 1 trip), use of vaguely defined, inconsistent or poorly executed methods. Unknown to ill defined; inconsistent or poorly executed. Uncertain numeric comparisons, but high reliability for presence or absence.

label: Type-6, Presence or Absence
definition: Non-numeric, year-specific record of presence/not detected (i.e., present/absent without estimating a count), tied to an explicit time and location, with reliable species identification. Underlying field methods may be highly variable; analysis is not required.

label: Upper benchmark
definition: An upper-threshold benchmark value, typically delineating the boundary between Amber and Green status zones.

label: Upper biological benchmark
definition: Upper benchmark value for a metric used to delineate Wild Salmon Policy status zones (typically the boundary between Amber and Green zones).

label: Upper stock reference
definition: A threshold below which fishing must be reduced to avoid reaching the LRP. The USR must be set at an appropriate distance above the LRP to provide sufficient opportunity for managers to recognize a declining stock status and sufficient time for management actions to take effect.

label: Uptime
definition: Counting/device uptime < 95% (or <90% with interpolation)

label: Visibility
definition: Poor visibility conditions

label: Visual Ground Count
definition: Visual surveys conducted by stream/bank walk with documented visit schedule, reach coverage, timing, and visibility conditions.

label: Visual Snorkel Count
definition: Two or more trained snorkelers conduct standardized passes within defined segments, tallying live/dead by species. Field forms capture visibility class, segment boundaries, and repeat-visit schedule; counts are aggregated across observers/passes.

label: Wild Salmon Policy
definition: Conservation Unit based stock assessment methods, metrics, and metadata

label: Wild Salmon Policy
definition: Canada's Wild Salmon Policy framework for conserving and managing wild Pacific salmon, including integrated status assessments and biological benchmarks for Conservation Units.

label: Wild Salmon Policy biological status zone
definition: The biological status of a Conservation Unit (CU) will normally be based on the abundance and distribution of spawners in the unit, or proxies thereof. For each CU, higher and lower benchmarks will be defined that will delimit three status zones: Green, Amber, and Red. As spawner abundance decreases, a CU moves towards the lower status zone, and the extent of management intervention for conservation purposes will increase.

label: Wild Salmon Policy Status Zone
definition: Controlled vocabulary for biological status zones under the Wild Salmon Policy.

label: WSP rapid status
definition: The WSP rapid status assessment approach builds on previously completed WSP integrated status assessments. The WSP rapid status approach ensures statuses are scientifically objective, consistent, and comparable across BC/Yukon CUs. This approach is also relatively easy to implement, applicable to data rich and data poor CUs, and can be updated annually.

label: WSP Rapid Status Algorithm threshold
definition: Threshold value used by the rapid-status algorithm that may differ from expert-defined benchmarks to incorporate precautionary buffers.

label: Year basis
definition: A year-based temporal reference used to contextualize measurements or rates (e.g., brood year or catch year).
